demurer: adjective. comparative form of demure: more demure

dree: verb. To bear or endure (something); to put up with, to suffer, to undergo.

due: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umiray Dumaget Agta.
durr: interjection. Alternative form of duh (“indicating stupidity etc.”).
eme: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emerillon.
err: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Erre.
erred: verb. simple past and past participle of err
med: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Melpa.
meed: noun. A payment or recompense made for services rendered or in recognition of some achievement; reward; award.
mere: adjective. Just, only; no more than, pure and simple, neither more nor better than might be expected.
merer: adjective. comparative form of mere: more mere
mud: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mednyj Aleut.

murderer: noun. A person who commits murder.
mured: verb. simple past and past participle of mure
murre: noun. Any seabird of the genus Uria in the family Alcidae (the auks).

ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reed: noun. Any of various types of tall stiff perennial grass-like plants growing together in groups near water.
rude: adjective. Lacking in refinement or civility; bad-mannered; discourteous.
ruder: adjective. comparative form of rude: more rude
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
rued: verb. simple past and past participle of rue
rum: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Romanian.
